---
title: "Chapter 19: Semi-Structured Interviews"
description: "Detailed study notes for Chapter 19 of Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 19
source_pages: "538-551"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch19-chapter-19-semi-structured-interviews:c0001"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Chapter 19</span><span>Source pages 538-551</span></div>

This note summarises Chapter 19, "Semi-Structured Interviews," from Kathryn E. Newcomer, Harry P.
Hatry, and Joseph S. Wholey's *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation*
[@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note
based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a substitute for the chapter.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source records at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the chapter's argument and procedures, and it does not reproduce textbook tables, checklists, figures, boxes, or extended passages.

## Core Argument

Semi-structured interviews help evaluators learn from knowledgeable respondents while preserving enough consistency for analysis.

## Study Summary

The chapter focuses on open-ended, person-to-person interviews used in field studies and case
studies.

It covers arranging interviews, developing guides, probing, recording, analysis, and reporting.

The main risk is relying on informal conversation without enough preparation, documentation, or
analytic structure.

Within the wider book, this chapter belongs to Part 2: Practical Data Collection Procedures. It
should be read alongside the neighboring chapters because the handbook treats design, data
collection, analysis, and use as connected evaluation work.

## Key Concepts

- **semistructured interview**: An interview guided by prepared topics while preserving open-ended probing and adaptation.
- **fieldwork**: Systematic in-person inquiry using interviews, observations, documents, and site-level evidence.
- **qualitative analysis**: Systematic coding, display, interpretation, and verification of qualitative data.

## Chapter Structure

- Design problem: Semi-structured interviews help evaluators learn from knowledgeable respondents while preserving enough consistency for analysis.
- Evidence focus: The chapter focuses on open-ended, person-to-person interviews used in field studies and case studies.
- Practice method: It covers arranging interviews, developing guides, probing, recording, analysis, and reporting.
- Quality risk: The main risk is relying on informal conversation without enough preparation, documentation, or analytic structure.

## Practical Implications

- Prepare interview guides around evaluation questions.
- Use probes without leading respondents.
- Plan how interview data will be coded and reported.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Chapter 18: Using the Internet](chapter-18-using-the-internet.qmd)
- Next: [Chapter 20: Focus Group Interviewing](chapter-20-focus-group-interviewing.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Evaluation Planning and Design](section-01-planning-and-design.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations](section-02-planning-useful-evaluations.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation](section-03-performance-measurement.qmd)

## Study Prompts

- What decision or design problem does Chapter 19 help resolve?
- Which evidence source should be reopened before using this summary in applied work?
- Which quality risk in the chapter is most likely to affect a live evaluation?

## Source Records

- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch19-chapter-19-semi-structured-interviews:c0001`
- `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-2:c0001`

## References
